methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
The Boring Company is a late-stage private transportation firm founded by Elon Musk, currently valued at $5.7B post-money on $675M in total capital raised. The relevant peer universe comprises four private Transportation companies—Rivian, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—with no public comparables identified. No narrow peer set met the threshold of three or more same-stage or adjacent-stage comps with sufficient valuation similarity, precluding a reliable peer-median methodology.

Stage-progression analysis, the sole applicable triangulation method, applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for late-stage advancement to the $5.7B base valuation, yielding $6.8B. This method rests on the assumption that the company progresses through its next funding round at a rate consistent with historical late-stage step patterns. The analysis does not incorporate comparable trading multiples or revenue-based metrics, given the absence of sufficiently narrow peer data and the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target.

The valuation analysis supports a range of $5.8B to $7.9B, centered on a median of $6.8B. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodology and highlights material uncertainty around appropriate step-up factors for this specific firm and sector. Key drivers of upside or downside include execution on capital intensity, market adoption rates, and competitive positioning relative to the identified peer set. Additional disclosed financial metrics would improve the robustness of this assessment.
